sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: CARDANO BLOCKCHAIN ECOSYSTEM CONSTITUTION v2.0
Stated amount: not stated in document

Deliverables: CARDANO BLOCKCHAIN ECOSYSTEM CONSTITUTION v2.0: https://cardano.mypinata.cloud/ipfs/bafkreigr35rigmubdmv5u7vr47d2tmvhq3i245uvtmzuciyexepcvaargu For a complete list of changes, see "List of changes from v1.0 to v2.0"., Here is a list of the major changes: Preamble / Article I / Article III / Appendix II: Minor wording improvements., Defined Terms: New definitions added for clarity., Removed the Budget info action requirement for treasury withdrawals.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): CARDANO BLOCKCHAIN ECOSYSTEM CONSTITUTION v2.0: https://cardano.mypinata.cloud/ipfs/bafkreigr35rigmubdmv5u7vr47d2tmvhq3i245uvtmzuciyexepcvaargu For a complete list of changes, see "List of changes from v1.0 to v2.0".
Claim (other, supported_in_proposal, low materiality): Here is a list of the major changes: Preamble / Article I / Article III / Appendix II: Minor wording improvements.
Claim (other, supported_in_proposal, low materiality): Defined Terms: New definitions added for clarity.
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Removed the Budget info action requirement for treasury withdrawals.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The current Constitution has six key issues: 1) Typos and inconsistent spelling 2) Redundant and unclear wording 3) Vague or missing definitions 4) Unused provisions (e.g., DRep/SPO Codes of Conduct) 5) Unnecessary budget requirement for treasury withdrawals

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): We addressed these issues as follows: 1) Fixed typos and cleaned up wording 2) Simplified structure and reduced word count 3) Clarified key definitions 4) Removed unused or outdated clauses 5) Removed the budget requirement for treasury withdrawals
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "CARDANO BLOCKCHAIN ECOSYSTEM CONSTITUTION v2.0: https://cardano.mypinata.cloud/ipfs/bafkreigr35rigmubdmv5u7vr47d2tmvhq3i245uvtmzuciyexepcvaargu For a complete list of changes, see "List of changes from v1.0 to v2.0"." and clears the evidence gates.
Strongest NO: weak controls, unclear delivery, or governance precedent could outweigh the benefit of NewConstitution.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
